channel.title: The QuantLab Blog
channel.description: Selected public writing and short-form essays connected to QuantLab research.

item.title: BLOG TEST 1: Bayesian inference beyond chronology
item.description: Bayesian methods in archaeology are useful for much more than chronology. They let us formalize competing hypotheses, compare evidence, and quantify uncertainty directly. Originally published at .
item.author: blog@thequantlab.org (Erik Otárola-Castillo)

item.title: Volunteer Profile
item.description: A short reflection on service, professional community, and why volunteering matters within archaeology. Originally published at SAA Archaeological Record.
item.author: blog@thequantlab.org (Erik Otárola-Castillo)

item.title: Monico Origins, a Bayesian Story
item.description: A public-facing account of how the Monico Bayesian story emerged and why simple narrative examples can help explain Bayesian inference in archaeology. Originally published at Cambridge Core Blog.
item.author: blog@thequantlab.org (Erik Otárola-Castillo)

item.title: 3D scanning: we recreated a sacred South African site in a way that captures its spirit
item.description: A short piece on digital reconstruction, place, and how 3D methods can communicate archaeological landscapes beyond conventional site photography. Originally published at The Conversation.
item.author: blog@thequantlab.org (Ben Schoville and collaborators)

item.title: Ancient southern Kalahari was more important to human evolution than previously thought
item.description: An accessible explanation of why the southern Kalahari matters for human evolutionary history and how new archaeological evidence reshapes older assumptions. Originally published at The Conversation.
item.author: blog@thequantlab.org (Ben Schoville and collaborators)
